name: Star of david
shortDescription: The Star of David, or Magen David, is a six-pointed hexagram generally recognized as a symbol of Judaism and Jewish identity. Its origins and early symbolism are debated, but it has become a prominent emblem in Jewish culture and tradition.
description: The Star of David, also known as the Magen David (Shield of David), is a hexagram composed of two equilateral triangles superimposed over each other. While it is almost universally recognized today as a symbol of Judaism, its origins and early significance are somewhat obscure. Archaeological evidence suggests that hexagrams were used in various cultures throughout history, sometimes for decorative purposes and other times with occult or magical associations. The earliest clear Jewish usage of the symbol is found on a seal from the 7th century BCE. However, its widespread adoption as a specifically Jewish emblem is a more recent phenomenon, dating back to the Middle Ages. The association with King David and the shield he supposedly carried appears to have developed later, possibly as a way to imbue the symbol with historical and religious significance. In Kabbalah, the two triangles are sometimes interpreted as representing the dual nature of humanity (good and evil) or the connection between the earthly and the divine realms. The upward-pointing triangle can symbolize humanity striving towards God, while the downward-pointing triangle can symbolize God's grace descending upon humanity. In modern times, the Star of David gained even greater prominence when it was adopted as the central symbol on the flag of the State of Israel in 1948, solidifying its place as a powerful and recognizable emblem of Jewish identity and nationhood. However, it's important to acknowledge that the symbol's meaning and significance have evolved over time and continue to be interpreted in various ways within Jewish thought and tradition. Its use in Jewish contexts has broadened from decorative and magical to religious, cultural, and national representation.

sources.title: Kabbalah: Key Concepts in the Development of Jewish Mysticism
sources.author: Adolph Franck

sources.title: The Star of David: A History
sources.author: Gershom Scholem
